name: Päijänne Tavastia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Tavastian arms
the golden charges of the arms

description: A banner-format red flag charged centrally with the gold and silver provincial arms of Päijänne Tavastia.
history: Päijänne Tavastia (Päijät-Häme) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, drawing on the historic arms of the Häme province.

features.symbolism: a heraldic composition derived from the historic Häme arms, with elements specific to the lakeland region around Lake Päijänne; gold and silver charges on a red field.
